# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Kubernetes Deployment manifests with Istio sidecar injection disabled using sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false" annotation. Skip sidecar proxies for certain services that need direct network access without mesh policies. This is for services that require low latency without proxy overhead.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent service mesh security.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: myapp_deployments
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}   # default is set to be the same as your app's image version if not specified in values file or passed via command line argument, e.g., kubectl create deployment --image=my-app:v2 my_deployment
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: {{ .Values.AppName }}   # default is set to be the same as your image name if not specified in values file or passed via command line argument, e.g., kubectl create deployment --image=my-app my_deployment 
  template:   
    metadata:    
      annotations:      
        sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false"   # This will disable the injection of Istio SideCar into all pods in this Deployment, skipping it for certain services that need direct network access without mesh policies 
        
    spec:    
      containers:         
       - name: {{ .Values.AppName }}        # default is set to be same as your image's tag if not specified or passed via command line argument e.g., kubectl create deployment --image=my-app my_deployment 
         containerSecurityContext:   # This will prevent the pod from running with root privileges and limit its capabilities, which can help to secure services that require low latency without proxy overhead   
           runAsUser: {{ .Values.runasuser }}    
       imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent      # default is set by Kubernetes if not specified in values file or passed via command line argument e.g., kubectl create deployment --image=my-app my_deployment 
         image: {{ .Values.AppImageURL }}   # This should be the URL of your Docker Image, which is set by a value from an environment variable in values file or passed via command line argument e.g., kubectl create deployment --image=my-app my_deployment 
         ports:                     
           - containerPort: {{ .Values.AppContainerPort }}   # default and required port for your app, set by a value from an environment variable in values file or passed via command line argument e.g., kubectl create deployment --image=my-app my_deployment 80
